title: Understanding Terminal Velocity
text: Terminal velocity is the constant speed that a freely falling object eventually reaches when the resistance of the medium prevents further acceleration. In this case, the diamond reaches a terminal velocity of $$2.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Calculating 90% of Terminal Velocity
text: To find 90% of the terminal velocity, multiply the terminal velocity by $$0.9$$.

title: Calculate 90% of Terminal Velocity
text: What is 90% of the terminal velocity of $$2.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$?

title: Using the Drag Equation
text: The drag force can be calculated using the equation: $$F_d=\frac{1}{2} C_d A \rho v^2$$, where $$C_d$$ is the drag coefficient, A is the cross-sectional area, rho is the fluid density, and v is the velocity.

title: Relating Drag Force to Weight
text: At terminal velocity, the drag force equals the weight of the object. Use this relationship to find the distance fallen when the velocity is 90% of terminal velocity.

title: Calculate Distance Fallen
text: Using the relationship between drag force and weight, calculate the distance the diamond falls before reaching 90% of its terminal velocity.
